metadata.title: Tidal Forces, Not Mass, Kill You at a Black Hole
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = tidal force / differential pull / distance gradient; CRIMSON = total force / mass / misleading intuition
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no GR curvature, no Hawking radiation from gram-mass BH, no neutron star comparison. Wonder register.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: Earth pulls on you right now with the same total mass as a coin-sized black hole. On Earth: comfortable. At the black hole's surface: every cell in your body tears apart. Same mass, totally different experience — and it has nothing to do with mass.

card.copy: Tidal Forces, Not Mass, Kill You at a Black Hole

beats.narration_text: Newton's law: force equals G times M times m divided by r squared. The force depends on distance squared. From the center of Earth, your head and feet are both about 6371 kilometers away — a negligible difference.

production_viz.label: F = GMm/r² curve; two dots nearly coincident at r=6371km for head and feet; negligible force difference labeled in TEAL

beats.narration_text: An Earth-mass black hole has identical mass to Earth. Why does Earth feel gentle while the same mass compressed to a coin tears every cell apart?

card.copy: Same mass: Earth and a coin-sized BH. Earth: comfortable. BH: every cell tears. What is the critical difference?
card.sub: Tidal force is the difference that kills

beats.narration_text: An Earth-mass black hole has a Schwarzschild radius of 9 millimeters. If you stood with your feet 1 centimeter from the center, your head would be about 181 centimeters away. Those two distances enter the force formula as r squared in the denominator.

production_viz.label: Coin-sized BH at center; person standing 1cm away for feet and 181cm for head labeled on F ∝ 1/r² curve; CRIMSON for extreme force difference

beats.narration_text: Force on feet: proportional to 1 over 1 centimeter squared. Force on head: proportional to 1 over 181 centimeters squared. The ratio is 181 squared over 1 squared — about 32,761. Your head is pulled 32,761 times less hard than your feet.

production_viz.label: Force bars: feet force vs head force at ratio 32,761:1; CRIMSON for catastrophic differential; equation (181/1)²=32,761 shown

beats.narration_text: The tidal force is the difference between these two pulls. On Earth, head and feet are both at 6371 kilometers — the ratio is 1.00003. Effectively no difference. On the Earth-mass black hole: a factor of 32,761. Every cell is shredded.

production_viz.label: Two columns: Earth (ratio 1.00003, TEAL safe) vs BH (ratio 32,761, CRIMSON lethal); tidal force = difference

beats.narration_text: Tidal force scales as GM over r cubed — not r squared. It grows faster as you approach. Small black holes are far more lethal than large ones at the event horizon.

production_viz.label: Tidal force ∝ GM/r³ curve steeply rising; small BH vs large BH comparison at event horizon showing small BH more lethal; CRIMSON for danger zone

beats.narration_text: A supermassive black hole like TON 618 has a Schwarzschild radius of 1300 AU. At that distance, tidal forces across a human body are negligible. You could cross the event horizon without feeling anything.

production_viz.label: TON 618 event horizon at 1300 AU scale; human body tiny by comparison; negligible tidal force labeled in TEAL

beats.narration_text: Spaghettification: the stretching of an infalling object by tidal forces. At what distance this becomes lethal depends entirely on black hole mass. For a stellar-mass BH: lethal far outside the event horizon. For TON 618: lethal deep inside.

production_viz.label: Spaghettification diagram: small BH lethal distance outside event horizon (CRIMSON) vs TON 618 lethal distance deep inside (TEAL)

beats.narration_text: Tidal force proportional to GM over r cubed. Same mass, different radius: Earth at 6371 kilometers versus Earth-mass BH at 9 millimeters. Force ratio across a body: Earth 1.00003, BH 32,761. The difference kills you, not the mass.

card.copy: Tidal force ∝ GM/r³. Earth-mass BH: r_s = 9 mm. Feet at 1 cm, head at 181 cm: force ratio = 32,761. Difference = spaghettification. TON 618: smooth crossing at event horizon.
card.sub: The difference kills you, not the mass

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
